hypothesis: Raw gamma=1.65 is a small-sample artifact from sparse high-k tail. Robust gamma (n>=5) remains sublinear ~0.5-0.8. Age-dependent attachment exists: lessons within 30 sessions get cited 2-3x more. Era-dependent gamma confirmed.
method: 1) Rerun pa_kernel.py on 662 lessons (vs 609 at S369). 2) Robust gamma: filter kernel rates to k where n_attachments>=5, >=3, >=10. Also cap at k<=15. 3) Era split at median session S325: measure gamma early vs late. 4) Age-dependent attachment: compute session-gap distribution and normalize by gap-bucket exposure. 5) Saturation: compare rate bands k=1-5, k=6-12, k=13-19.

raw_all_k.note: ARTIFACT: n=1 at k>=20 with rates 0.1-0.5 inflate slope

era_dependent.interpretation: PA kernel emerged only in late era. Early citations were approximately uniform across available degrees.

age_dependent_attachment.note: 27x proximity effect far exceeds the 2-3x predicted. Session proximity is the DOMINANT citation mechanism.

saturation.note: Rates rise to k=12, then decline. Saturation above k~12.

zero_inflation.note: Still strong zero-inflation. First citation is the hardest to get.

expect: gamma still sublinear 0.5-0.8. BIC inconclusive. Age effect 2-3x within 30 sessions. Era effect: different gamma early vs late.
actual: Robust gamma=0.63-0.71 (CONFIRMED sublinear). Raw gamma=1.65 is artifact. BIC DELTA=1.45 (still inconclusive). Age effect 27x (FAR stronger than 2-3x predicted). Era: early gamma=0 (flat), late gamma=0.56 (sublinear). Saturation peak at k=12.
diff: gamma CONFIRMED sublinear when measured correctly — S381 recent gamma=1.89 is same artifact (n=1 high-k tail). Age effect 27x vs 2-3x predicted (order of magnitude wrong). Era effect confirmed but early era is FLAT not just different-gamma. Saturation discovered (not predicted).
verdict: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED. Three of four predictions correct in direction, one (age 2-3x) was correct in direction but off by 10x in magnitude. The critical methodological finding: raw pa_kernel.py gamma is unreliable due to sparse high-k tail. All prior gamma measurements using raw log-log regression over full k range need reinterpretation.
